commentary: Okay so eleven activities last week, ten this week, which on paper looks like a step back but honestly the quality here tells a different story — that half marathon run came in at 159 bpm average versus 151 last week on the same distance, which means you pushed harder, not softer, and that is very much the energy we are going for. Three full 2000m pool sessions plus a cheeky open water Friday at Balmoral is giving very "I swim in the actual ocean now" and he is absolutely right to. The one thing worth watching is Wednesday disappearing entirely again — there is a hole in the middle of your week that keeps showing up, and a body that trains this hard deserves something in that slot even if it is gentle. That said, you ended the week lifting at Virgin on a Friday afternoon, which means you did not coast into the weekend, and that detail does not go unnoticed.
metrics_commentary: Okay that swim is genuinely unhinged in the best way — 6670 metres is not a casual splash around, that is a man who moves through water like he has somewhere important to be, probably somewhere with good lighting. The single run at 13.1 km tells me you went out once and just decided to half-marathon it, which is very much a choice, and honestly a correct one. Your HRV climbing to 61 ms against a seven-day average of 52 is your body basically confirming that you are recovered, capable, and ready to do something stupid and fun with all that energy. The VO2 Max sitting at 56.4 is legitimately excellent and means your engine is big, so the only real note is that one run session is a little lonely — your cardiovascular system is clearly built for more, and it would love the company.
gym_commentary: Four out of four sessions, babe, the programme is not a suggestion and apparently you know that now. Four PRs in a single week is genuinely unhinged in the best possible way — legs pressing 168 kilos, that cable row climbing to 84, the hammer curls jumping from 16.8 to 19.6 like they had somewhere important to be. Volume came in lower at just over 20,000 kilos versus last week's 23,660, but that's not a red flag, that's your body cashing the cheque your intensity wrote. The strength numbers are going up, the body doing the lifting is becoming something, and I am keeping very close tabs on all of it.
